# UC-14.3.60 · Aranet Dew Point from Temperature and Relative Humidity

## Description

Condensation in data halls and labs follows dew point, not RH alone. Magnus-formula dew point computed from Aranet temperature and humidity predicts surface condensation risk when chilled water temperatures or door seals drift.

## Value

Protects equipment and commodities from moisture damage by alerting on psychrometric risk before water forms on racks or packaging.

## Implementation

Ensure `temp_c` and `humidity_pct` are numeric. The SPL uses the common Magnus approximation over water (good for facility monitoring). Tune thresholds: data centers often target dew point well below room dry bulb; adjust `dew_c > 15` to your architecture. Document sensor calibration cadence per QA.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Validate formula against handheld psychrometer quarterly.
• Exclude outdoor canopy sensors if mixed index.

Step 1 — Add `case()` for Fahrenheit sites converting to Celsius first.

Step 2 — Correlate with chilled water supply temp if ingested.

Step 3 — SOP for facilities when alert fires.

Step 4 — Optional ITSI thresholding.

## SPL

```spl
index=environment sourcetype=aranet:sensor
| eval T=tonumber(temp_c), RH=tonumber(humidity_pct)
| eval a=(17.27*T)/(237.7+T)
| eval dew_c=round((237.7*a+log(RH/100.0))/(17.27-a),2)
| where dew_c > 15 AND RH > 65
| table _time, sensor_id, location, T, RH, dew_c
| sort - dew_c
```

## Visualization

Scatter (RH vs dew point), Line chart (dew_c trend), Heatmap (room × time).
